paragraphs.body: On this day it was 90 degrees, humid, and I walking down Cardiac hill headed to the School of Public Health. I was almost overwhelmed by the loud bangs from the construction, horns beeping, and seeing students speed walking to not be late. I could feel my calves begin to tighten and my skin became sticky because of the humidity. When I entered through the front doors I was immediately hit with the dead silence of the building. I heard not even a whisper. As I turned the left corner there was a pair of glass doors that I could see straight through and as I pulled them open, I could hear the rubber on the bottom break the silence just a little bit by scrapping against the tile. I then sunk into the spongy chair to wait for my advisor meeting. I heard a soft voice reminding me that I need to sign in, so I walked over to check in. As I watched my key card swipe, the lady at the front desk offered me candy, so I walked over to her desk and saw a little candy bowl. As I could hear the rustling of the candy wrappers, I finally saw one that appealed to me the most. The one I chose had a light pink and red wrapper. Before I saw what the candy was called, I heard a voice say, “Paisley,” so I threw the candy into the left pocket of my bookbag and followed my advisor back to her office. After my meeting, I began to walk back up cardiac hill, which made my both of my legs to start aching, so to distract myself I decided to pull out the piece of candy. After digging in my pocket, feeling past the old, sticky gum wrappers, and the cold loose change, I found the candy that read, “Strawberry Crème Saver.” I took it out of the wrapper and placed it on my tongue. I immediately tasted the sweet strawberry flavor that tings on my tongue, mixed with what feels like a soft crème that mixes perfectly with the strawberry. As soon as I got off the second set of the escalators in the Peterson Event Center, the saver was gone. I immediately needed another one. It’s like it released dopamine in my brain. It was as if in that moment of time my worries were gone, and I was just on my way back to my dorm enjoying the present.

paragraphs.body: The word “purpose” stands as a noun. According to the Etymology Dictionary, the definition is “intention, aim, goal; object to be kept in view; proper function for which something exists.” The word “purpose” relates to everyone, no matter their age, race, income status, we all have a purpose to do something in this world and that’s one of the things that connects everyone. A purpose may be something you say, give, how you treat somebody, etc. I believe that everyone has more than one purpose in life, no matter how little or large those purposes maybe they all have an impact. A purpose should not be intended to harm someone, however, maybe if some you love causes harm in your life there is a deeper purpose for all the pain you may experience. My purpose is to prove to everyone that I am good enough despite coming from a stigmatized background. My purpose is to get a degree from Pitt in Psychology and go on in life to heal people. My purpose is to be rich in life, not rich in money, but rich in love. My purpose is to have children that will be raised much different than I was, and I will protect them from the things that I was exposed to as a child. My purpose is to make sure my children have the most support in the world and never have to be worried about losing me. My purpose is to forgive my parents, because all the trauma I experienced made me who I am today. My purpose is to prove to people no matter what background you come from; you can be somebody. Don’t let anyone tell you who you can or can’t be. Stand to the people that think their better than you because they drive a nice car and you ride a bus, stand up to those people who judge you because your parents were never married, stand up to those people that judge you because you live with your grandmother, and stand up to those people who make you feel like you aren’t good enough. My purpose is to make a beautiful life for myself no matter what it takes.

paragraphs.body: When I first applied to the University of Pittsburgh, I was so nervous I wasn’t going to be accepted. I constantly was anxious about my grades and worried I wasn’t going to be good enough. As a first-generation college student, the college application process was more than stressful. I was constantly concerned about how I was going to afford attending college. I applied during early admissions because I wanted to be ahead. I was a product of a teen pregnancy and single mother. I was also born into a family of addiction, which constantly made me doubt myself that I would never be good enough and never escape the stigma. Even though we had no idea what we were doing, my family was beyond supportive of me and pushed me to pursue my dreams of getting into the university. They constantly reassured me that everything will work out, and even if I don’t get in everything happens for a reason. I remember telling myself that if I did not get in, I just wouldn’t tell anybody and go to a different university. I spent countless nights crying, worried that I will be no one in this world and everyone will expect me to fail because of my homelife. I decided to just not think about it, so I quit checking the application portal and accepted my fate that I wasn’t going to get in.

annotations.comment: The  details throughout this paragraph are so good. The context you provide here is powerful.
annotations.context_left: enough. As a first-generation college student, the college application process was more than

paragraphs.body: On September 16, 2023, I was sitting on my boyfriend’s couch with him and his dad watching the backyard brawl. I had on my favorite Pitt hoodie that I wore every week. I decided to check the application portal because I hadn’t in about a week. I opened it up to see another letter, my heart started beating out of my chest I could feel it in my throat. I eagerly opened the letter. My hands were shaking. I was accepted. I instantly started crying and ran into their basement while calling my mom. She began to cry hysterically telling me how proud she was of me. She called everyone in her phone to tell them that I got in. I thought to myself, I really did it despite everything. I came to Pitt not knowing what to expect. The first month in college was a learning process. I had no clue what time to leave for classes, where I was going, who to talk to, I wanted to throw up. All I wanted to do was just fit in and be like everyone else. I saw girls walking around with expensive, trendy bags, girls with huge friend groups during the first week, and people with brand new tech things, I felt so weird. I had a huge lump in my throat every time I walked into my classrooms trying to find somewhere to sit. I was so scared I wasn’t going to make friends or get good grades. Everything was so unfamiliar. I just wanted to feel like I belonged and now today I know that Pitt is my home. I finally feel like I am right where I need to be, I have formed an amazing friend group, I have an amazing support system, and I know that I don’t need to have the nicest things to be somebody. I can be myself and that will always be good enough, and I know this now because on one person that took the time out of their day to make me feel like I belonged.

paragraphs.body: Today, when I enter in the School of Public Health, I take a left I wait to see the kind lady working the front desk, but if she is not there, I ring the bell. She turns the corner with a big smile on her face and asks, “You’re here for the strawberry crème candies, aren’t you?” And I respond by saying “Yes, I am.” She and the other people that work in there now know who I am because I always stop in and say hello and grab a strawberry crème saver. But on this specific day I came in, she told me she had something for me. She pulled out a roll of strawberry crème savers and told me that the lady that buys her them bought me some too. This absolutely made my day. These little moments in my life have a purpose I cherish being able to go to such an amazing college and meeting such kind people. I know that when I’m having a bad day, I can go into the student affairs office and be treated with nothing but kindness. The sad thing is still since I started college in August, I still do not know her name, but I could never not recognize her face. She has made me feel like I belong here at the University of Pittsburgh. She has made some of my bad days turn better and brought a smile to my face just by serving a little purpose in my life. I think everyone needs to talk a step back and appreciate the moments in life that are full of purpose. If you start to pay attention, you’ll notice them too.
